jokes.text: So how much are they paying you? Nothing. Uh, as a matter of fact, they treat me like a shoeshine boy.
jokes.setup: Question about payment for prestigious TV work
jokes.punchline: Not only not paid, but treated like menial labor
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - Jim's naive acceptance of exploitation sets up the episode's theme

jokes.text: I can't accept any money. Uh... you can pay me for the shoeshine.
jokes.setup: Jim nobly refuses payment for his valuable TV insights
jokes.punchline: But will accept payment for literal shoe shining
jokes.explanation: Excellent character logic - Jim's twisted priorities perfectly capture his innocent worldview

jokes.text: How much do I owe you? Taxi. $150,000.
jokes.setup: Simple question about shoe shine cost
jokes.punchline: Astronomical amount for basic service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the episode's setup - Jim finds his own bizarre way to value his work

jokes.text: Okay, here goes. I'm gonna have to fire you.
jokes.setup: Janine psyching herself up for something unpleasant with encouragement
jokes.punchline: The 'unpleasant thing' is firing the person helping her
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but not particularly character-specific or memorable

jokes.text: You can't live with 'em, you can't live without 'em.
jokes.setup: TV executive just fired someone in his cab
jokes.punchline: Jim treats it like casual relationship trouble
jokes.explanation: Jim's oblivious reaction to the drama he just witnessed is in character but predictable

jokes.text: In my business, you can be burnt out by the time your skin clears up.
jokes.setup: Explaining how young the TV business is
jokes.punchline: Burn out before acne clears - extreme youth emphasis
jokes.explanation: Well-crafted line about TV industry youth culture, good economy of language

jokes.text: Oh, then how come you took off Star Trek? What? That was my favorite show.
jokes.setup: TV executive explaining scientific programming decisions
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately challenges with personal grievance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - ignoring the big picture for personal concerns, very character-specific

jokes.text: The only guy I didn't like was the leader of the Romulans... They gave him things to say that no Romulan would ever say.
jokes.setup: Jim criticizing Star Trek
jokes.punchline: Deeply specific critique about Romulan dialogue authenticity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - Jim has inexplicably deep knowledge of fictional alien psychology

jokes.text: The Pittsburgh Steelers at Marineland.
jokes.setup: Musical variety show that's 'trouble'
jokes.punchline: Completely bizarre combination of football team and marine park
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd TV show concept that perfectly captures bad 70s variety programming

jokes.text: You don't tell me how to program television shows, and I won't tell you how to drive a cab, okay?
jokes.setup: Mitch dismissing Jim's TV predictions
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by Jim driving wrong way
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate comeuppance - well-structured comedic irony

jokes.text: Why are you heading into the exit of the Lincoln Tunnel? Oh, oh, oh! Remember our deal.
jokes.setup: Mitch's deal about not telling each other their jobs
jokes.punchline: Jim uses it to deflect from dangerous driving error
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate payoff to the previous setup - Jim's literal interpretation is both dangerous and funny

jokes.text: It'll never work. He brings his own lunch.
jokes.setup: Rat poison for Louie
jokes.punchline: Won't work because Louie brings lunch (implying he won't eat garage food)
jokes.explanation: Good character-based joke about Louie being too paranoid/careful to fall for poison

jokes.text: Jim tasting the white powder and everyone's horrified reaction
jokes.setup: White powder that might be rat poison
jokes.punchline: Jim casually tastes it while others watch in horror
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim physical comedy - his obliviousness to danger creates tension and release

jokes.text: You just had a dance lesson?
jokes.setup: Louie asking what tiny footprints tell them
jokes.punchline: Alex suggests dance lesson instead of mouse tracks
jokes.explanation: Decent deflation of Louie's dramatic detective moment with mundane alternative

jokes.text: As soon as that helpless little mouse scurries across the floor, you know who's gonna be standing on that table, screeching like a banshee, don't you?
jokes.setup: Elaine arguing against cruel mousetraps
jokes.punchline: Louie pointing out she'll be the one screaming on furniture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Louie calling out the contradiction in Elaine's mouse sympathy

jokes.text: You always know how to appeal to my finer instincts, Nardo.
jokes.setup: Elaine convincing Louie by mentioning saving money
jokes.punchline: Louie calling greed his 'finer instincts'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - self-aware about his greed while calling it noble

jokes.text: Oh. Raymond Burr!
jokes.setup: TV executive says 'Do you remember me? I'm the guy in television'
jokes.punchline: Jim assumes he's famous actor Raymond Burr
jokes.explanation: Good Jim logic - anyone in television must be Raymond Burr, shows his simple thinking

jokes.text: Jim, if you had had this chance a few years ago, you could have saved Star Trek.
jokes.setup: Jim refusing to help with TV programming
jokes.punchline: Appeal to his earlier Star Trek obsession
jokes.explanation: Good callback that uses Jim's established obsession to motivate him

jokes.text: Shall we boldly go where no man has ever gone before?
jokes.setup: Convincing Jim to help with TV programming
jokes.punchline: Using Star Trek catchphrase to seal the deal
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective use of Star Trek reference to manipulate Jim

jokes.text: I need to take the rest of the afternoon off. I'm gonna go save television.
jokes.setup: Simple request for time off
jokes.punchline: Grandiose mission statement about saving television
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim grandiosity - taking simple TV consulting and making it epic

jokes.text: Oh, I suppose a periodontist is out.
jokes.setup: Trying to find professional identity for Jim
jokes.punchline: Periodontist ruled out (implying Jim doesn't look professional enough)
jokes.explanation: Mild class-based humor about Jim's appearance not fitting professional roles

jokes.text: Oh, I'm, uh, just looking over the schedule before I shine his shoes.
jokes.setup: Secretary walking in on Jim examining TV schedule
jokes.punchline: Jim explains he studies programming before shoe shining
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - Jim treating shoe shining as serious business requiring TV knowledge

jokes.text: Yeah, but under the bed. Not on your head.
jokes.setup: Jim looking at helmet for game show
jokes.punchline: Noting unusual location for protective headwear
jokes.explanation: Jim's innocent observation about safety equipment placement is mildly amusing

jokes.text: What's in that big tank over Paul Lynde's head? Melted cheese.
jokes.setup: Game show with consequences for wrong answers
jokes.punchline: Celebrity will be doused in melted cheese
jokes.explanation: Good absurd game show concept that captures the era's cruel variety shows

jokes.text: Hey, don't feel bad. If you could do your job, you would!
jokes.setup: Mitch feeling bad about letting cabdriver do his job
jokes.punchline: Jim's brutally honest 'comfort'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim line - attempting comfort while delivering devastating honesty about incompetence

jokes.text: Don't get encouraged, Nardo. Just because Banta fell for it, doesn't mean a mouse will.
jokes.setup: Elaine impressed with humane mousetrap
jokes.punchline: Louie implying Tony is dumber than a mouse
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie insult - using Tony falling for the trap to insult his intelligence

jokes.text: It's right there in your hand. Jim! It's a phone call for you!
jokes.setup: Jim apparently not understanding he's holding a phone
jokes.punchline: Alex having to over-explain the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy moment with Jim's obliviousness to obvious things

jokes.text: If you can't tell me how happy you are, why did you call?
jokes.setup: Phone conversation about TV success
jokes.punchline: Jim's literal interpretation of emotional expression
jokes.explanation: Good Jim logic - misunderstanding figures of speech and taking them literally

jokes.text: Hey, it's thanks enough to me that he's walking around with a nice shine on his shoes. Every time he gets a compliment on his shoes, it reflects on me.
jokes.setup: Others saying Jim should demand payment for TV work
jokes.punchline: Jim satisfied with shoe shine recognition, 'reflects' pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim character moment - his twisted priorities and innocent pun about reflection

jokes.text: Some people in this world make themselves victims, and Jim is one of them.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jim being taken advantage of
jokes.punchline: Alex's blunt psychological assessment
jokes.explanation: Alex's frustrated analysis of Jim's self-defeating behavior is character-appropriate

jokes.text: When do you finally stand up and assert yourself? August.
jokes.setup: Alex asking rhetorical question about self-assertion
jokes.punchline: Jim gives specific calendar month as literal answer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim moment - completely literal response to rhetorical question. Perfect character voice and timing.

jokes.text: And not just because I'm outta black.
jokes.setup: Jim declaring he won't shine shoes anymore for dignity reasons
jokes.punchline: Also mentions being out of black shoe polish
jokes.explanation: Good Jim deflation - undermining his dignified stance with mundane practical concern

jokes.text: Boy, I bet they say some cute things. They do. Like what? Well, I can't think of anything now...
jokes.setup: Mitch showing photos of his children
jokes.punchline: Can't actually recall any cute sayings when asked
jokes.explanation: Mild awkward moment - common parental cliche deflated by inability to deliver examples

jokes.text: My hairdresser... My nephews picked our 6:00 news team.
jokes.setup: Justifying getting advice from random people
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd sources of TV programming decisions
jokes.explanation: Good satire of TV industry's random decision-making process

jokes.text: I love champagne... And-and do they have shrimp?... Those... I love those big shrimp.
jokes.setup: Jim being told he can't come to the victory party
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly continuing to ask about party details
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Jim missing social cues and making rejection more awkward

jokes.text: My ability to see glimpses of the future is a gift from God and I refuse to abuse it or taint it by using it for financial gain.
jokes.setup: Jim refusing payment for TV work
jokes.punchline: Grandiose spiritual justification for basic TV predictions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim grandiosity - turning TV hunches into divine gift, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Uh, you can pay me for the shoe shine.
jokes.setup: Jim's noble refusal to profit from his gift
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks for payment for manual labor
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character consistency - Jim's twisted value system in action

jokes.text: I could flush you. Send you bodysurfing down the tubes. A little sewer snack for some alligator.
jokes.setup: Louie contemplating what to do with caught mouse
jokes.punchline: Elaborately cruel description of flushing the mouse
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - unnecessarily elaborate and dark threats to small creature

jokes.text: Just my luck you're a baby.
jokes.setup: Louie catching the mouse and deciding its fate
jokes.punchline: Frustrated that mouse is too young/cute to kill
jokes.explanation: Good Louie moment - showing his secret soft spot while maintaining gruff exterior

jokes.text: Tails! Surf's up!
jokes.setup: Coin flip to determine mouse's fate
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic death sentence with surfing metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good dark Louie humor - cheerful delivery of violent threat with surf imagery

jokes.text: Nah, I was only kiddin'. It's heads.
jokes.setup: Louie announcing mouse's death sentence
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was lying about the coin flip result
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation - Louie showing his hidden soft side through deception
